localizations.title: Sherlock Holmes The Burnt Angels Case
localizations.hiddenObject_2: Have you searched Master Fogel thoroughly?
localizations.hint_2_first: Before Red Henry turned around and walked away, you had time to observe 4 very specific elements: his pale complexion, his chewed jacket, his faded trousers, and the seagull feather hanging from his hat.
localizations.hint_2: The lack of exposure to sunlight, the frequent comings and goings through water, and proximity to rodents suggest that Red Henry is hiding in the sewers. Seagulls, attracted to fish, generally live near places where fishing boats dock.
localizations.hint_5_first: If you concentrate the sun's rays on the newspaper, you may be able to make fire.
localizations.hint_5: Place the magnifying glass to concentrate the sun's rays on the newspaper (5). A number can be seen in the visible area.
localizations.hint_12: One of the victims had to go through here. Which one?
localizations.hint_17: It looks like Red Henry no longer lives here, but he left a few leads to follow. The three pieces of paper left on the floor form a card number.
localizations.hint_20: According to the homeless (81), the gang members hide among them after their thefts. You may even have crossed paths with one of them earlier in the day.
localizations.hint_23: The door is locked, but you have what is required to blow the lock up.
localizations.hint_24: Edith Chadha came into contact with Red Henry somewhere between the Strand and Green Park. Once you know the route of the two other victims, look for a connection between the three routes.
localizations.hint_25: Use the magnifying glass to read the engraving on the ring: when placed over it, the head mount of the magnifying glass and the rings silver circles form a number.
localizations.hint_26_first: The coded message (83) will let you know where Master Fogel's train is coming from. The message shows 7 stopovers. Each of them provides a letter.
localizations.hint_26: The journey stopovers correspond to some of the cities represented in the map of Europe (M).
localizations.hint_28: The watchman may have some information about one of the victims, but which one?
localizations.hint_29: What can you use this on to blast your way to freedom?
localizations.hint_30: You could exchange these clothes for important information about your investigation.
localizations.hint_36: Which victim used a carriage every day to get to work?
localizations.hint_46: Red Henry has taken revenge for the death of his parents. He killed the members of the medical staff who provided care to the wealthy spectators and left the members of HEAVEN’S CHOIR, including his parents, to die in the FLAMES.
localizations.hint_49: What can you show to this silversmith?
localizations.hint_50:
You need to find some evidence to make him talk.
Start by searching him.
localizations.hint_51: Until you find something to convince them, the homeless won't tell you anything.
localizations.hint_53: Find the intersecting location that each of the three routes has in common.
localizations.hint_54: Somewhere in the room, you will find what you need to cause the cartridge to explode (54).
localizations.hint_55: The piece of the map corresponds to a quarter of the London plan (P, L, A, or N). Next, cross-check the watchman's information (39) to deduce the location of Edith Chadha's apartment (victim #3).
localizations.hint_56: Oxford Street is the right street, but #2 seems to be a red herring. The dots under the address in the diary (75) will provide you with the correct number.
localizations.hint_61_first: The small stains on the cuff must have caught Sherlock's attention. But to the naked eye, it is difficult to identify the nature of those stains.
localizations.hint_61: Place the magnifying glass over the photograph of Victim #1 (61) to examine the stains on the cuff. The bloodstains form two numbers.
localizations.hint_63: Use the symbol on the cufflink as well as the chalk and antiseptic residues (63) to determine Victim #1's workplace.
localizations.hint_69: It is the address of the silversmith who made this ring. He may have information about Victim #2.
localizations.hint_73: John Koffley came into contact with Red Henry somewhere between College Street and University Hospital. Once you know the route of each of the two other victims, look for the common denominator for these three routes.
localizations.hint_74: Mr. Dray came into contact with Red Henry somewhere between Charing Cross and the shop on Saint James's Square. Once you know the route of each of the two other victims, look for the common denominator for these three routes. The poster behind the silversmith catches your attention.
localizations.hint_75: Find the loot and you'll have your proof to make Fogel talk. In regard to the address written in the diary; Oxford Street is the right address, but #2 seems to be a red herring. The dots under the address will provide the correct number.
localizations.hint_81: Ask the homeless where to go to track down the Fogel Gang.
localizations.hint_83: Master Fogel arrives today at the London train station (see the date in the newspaper(T)).
localizations.hint_85_first: Using the various elements found in Red Henry's hideout (17), you should be able to narrow down the suspects and find Red Henry.
localizations.hint_85: Red Henry's parents were members of a choir (46). He, himself, owns a violin (17) and seems to appreciate medieval ballads particularly.
localizations.hint_97: There is only one place close to a park, next to a lake, and at a 10-minute carriage ride from University Hospital.
localizations.hint_98: Try to learn more about this Jack Young. He will probably lead you to Red Henry's identity.
localizations.hint_100: John Koffley came by carriage every day. So, he was a regular client of some of the coachmen.
localizations.hint_200: The lead to be followed, which Sherlock refers to, is about the 3rd victim crime scene (11) and the suspicions hanging over the Fogel Gang. So go and investigate the crime scene to find out more.
localizations.answer_hint_2: Red Henry must be hiding in the sewers which are accessible from the banks of the Thames. On the map, spot a small entrance to a tunnel on the banks of the Thames. This is where Red Henry must be hiding. Go to Q4.
localizations.answer_hint_5: You can see TWENTY (on the top) NINE (on the bottom).
localizations.answer_hint_12: According to his wife (27), John Koffley went to Warlock Entertainers on the day he died. Question the owner (12) about Victim #1 (61).
localizations.answer_hint_17: When you piece together the gray speech bubble, you form the number 16.
localizations.answer_hint_20: Compare the individuals that were among the group of homeless in the morning (51) with those in the afternoon (81). All are present except the one who was leaning against the tree (51). However, that individual is present at Wellington Arch (20). Can you spot him?
localizations.answer_hint_23: The shotgun cartridge (31) could be used as an explosive. First, insert it in the keyhole (23).
localizations.answer_hint_25: The mount of the magnifying glass and the silver circles on the ring form the number 69.
localizations.answer_hint_26: Transpose the route of the coded message (83) onto the map of Europe (M): Barcelona, Rome, Innsbruck, Stockholm, Tallinn, Oslo, London. Take the first letter of each city to form the word BRISTOL. Go to platform 8.
localizations.answer_hint_28: Interrogate the Green Park watchman (28) about Victim #3 (11) who was found in his park.
localizations.answer_hint_29: Once you have placed the shotgun cartridge in the lock (54), use this flame (29) to blow up the lock.
localizations.answer_hint_30: Give these clothes (30) to the homeless (51).
localizations.answer_hint_36: Interrogate the coachman (36) about John Koffley, Victim #1 (61).
localizations.answer_hint_46: The Burnt Angels that Sherlock seeks are members of the Heaven's Choir who died in the fire. Bring this newspaper article to Sherlock (7).
localizations.answer_hint_48: Show the gang's loot (48) to Master Fogel (50) to obtain the information you need.
localizations.answer_hint_49: Show this silversmith (49) the ring (25) you found at Red Henry's.
localizations.answer_hint_50: Spot the hidden number on his coat.
localizations.answer_hint_55: The cross on the map indicates the location of Edith Chadha's apartment on one of the 4 quarters of the London map: C1, C6, L1, or L6. But according to the information provided by the watchman (39), this victim used to live close to a church. L6 is the only location next to a church. Investigate L6.
localizations.answer_hint_56: The dots indicate 51-20; Go to number 31.
localizations.answer_hint_61: The bloodstains form a 6 and a 3.
localizations.answer_hint_63: The symbol on the cufflink represents a hospital (18). The antiseptic residue (63) sustain the hypothesis that Victim #1 (61) is a physician. To which hospital should you go to? The chalk residue leads you to believe that this victim is also a lecturer. It is likely that Victim #1 is a lecturer of medicine. Go to University Hospital (K7).
localizations.answer_hint_69: Go and pay a visit to Degan Silversmith. Meet in Saint James's Square at I2.
localizations.answer_hint_73: Each route of the 3 victims (24, 73, and 74) cross Trafalgar Square. That is where Red Henry must come into contact with his victims. Try to flush him out by going to L3.
localizations.answer_hint_74: According to his wife, Victim #1, John Koffley, was going to Warlock Entertainers. You now have the address. Go to College Street at M0.
localizations.answer_hint_75: First go to Oxford Street at G5. For the street number, the dots indicate 51-20. In machine 56, enter number 31.
localizations.answer_hint_81: The grassroots of the Fogel Gang are at Wellington Arch. Use the button in the app to go to coordinates C0.
localizations.answer_hint_83: Start by going to Charing Cross train station at N3.
localizations.answer_hint_85: Red Henry's parents were singers and, he, himself, plays music. To spot his victims, Red Henry must regularly go to Trafalgar Square. If such is the case, he must be the street singer next to the fountain. Go and interrogate that individual.
localizations.answer_hint_97: On the map, going from University Hospital (K7), take to road northwest bound (J8), i.e., a 2-minute carriage ride. Then, follow that road westbound to B8 for an additional 8-minute ride. Here you are near a park, next to a lake, and at a 10-minute carriage ride from University Hospital. Use the button in the app to go to B8.
localizations.answer_hint_98: Go and investigate at the orphanage on Hopkins Street at J4. Ask to see Jack Young's file.
localizations.answer_hint_100: Go to the carriage stables at O8.
localizations.answer_hint_200: According to the newspaper (T), Victim #3 (11) was found at the north corner of Green Park, corresponding to coordinates F2. Use the button in the app to go there.
localizations.machine_20: You tail the bandit. He takes you to a nearby warehouse. You enter discreetly but as soon as you do, the door closes heavily behind you. You are trapped!
localizations.machine_26: You stand at the entrance to the platform, waiting for the train from Bristol to arrive. Once the train is at the platform, a crowd of people disembark from the wagons and rush towards you. Among them, the much-wanted gang leader, Master Fogel. Assisted by two policemen, you walk towards him...
localizations.machine_56: You go to 31 Oxford Street. You find what appears to be an empty house. After fiddling with the lock, you get inside.
localizations.machine_85: As you come close, Red Henry runs for his life, heading to The Strand. Faster than a speeding bullet, he sneaks among the many onlookers and shakes you off easily.
localizations.machine_special_A4: "Hello! Are you here for the Serpentine tour? Ok, get in line!" Unfortunately, it looks like nothing here can make the case progress one way or another!
localizations.machine_special_B6: The apartment door is ajar. You call out, but no one answers. You quietly enter…
localizations.machine_special_B8: You knock at the door of a house overlooking the beautiful lake of Regent's Park. A woman opens and lets you in.
localizations.machine_special_C0: As is often the case, Wellington Arch is bustling.
localizations.machine_special_F1: There is nothing in this part of the park.
localizations.machine_special_F2: Once in the park, you catch a glimpse of a silhouette through the mist.
localizations.machine_special_G5: You are now in the famous and very long Oxford Street.
localizations.machine_special_I2: You find Degan Silversmith in the area of Saint James's Square.
localizations.machine_special_J4: After a short wait, the director of the orphanage receives you in his office.
localizations.machine_special_K7: In the hospital hallway, you meet a nurse.
localizations.machine_special_L3: On the lookout for the killer, you discreetly pace around a bustling Trafalgar Square.
localizations.machine_special_L6: There is no one at Edith Chadha's. So, you knock at the door of a neighbor, and an old woman answers.
localizations.machine_special_M0: A stout man welcomes you in his shop.
localizations.machine_special_N3: You enter the noisy train station of Charing Cross.
localizations.machine_special_O4: The Strand is very busy. You won't find anything here.
localizations.machine_special_O8: Here you are, sneaking between carriages.
localizations.machine_special_error: It looks like nothing here can make the case progress one way or another!
localizations.machine_special_none: It looks like nothing here can make the case progress one way or another!
localizations.machine_special_N3_required: As long as you remain stuck in this warehouse, you can't go anywhere.
localizations.machine_14_step1_hint: Jack Young and Red Henry were together at the orphanage. You should determine the period during which Red Henry lived at the orphanage. All that is left to do is to guess a first or last name.
localizations.machine_14_step1_answer: First, try searching the name Jack Young and spot the dates of his stay at the orphanage, i.e., 1884-1887. Red Henry was an alias, but you can guess that his first name was probably Henry. Search the first name Henry for the period 1884-1887.
localizations.machine_14_step2_hint: Have you noticed the common denominator shared by Victims #1 and #3?
localizations.machine_14_step2_answer: John Koffley (Victim #1) and Edith Chadha (Victim #3) were both health professionals. One of the 4 children refused to be examined by a physician. Chances are it is linked to the clipping of the newspaper mentioning the Burnt Angels (T). Continue your investigation and go to Henry Evans' former address, Manchester Square at B6.
localizations.machine_special_title: Where do you wish to go?
localizations.lestrade_1:
After Sherlock Holmes, here come his henchmen. Your mentor just left.
He drew my attention to the victim’s sleeve. Per him, the residues would allow identifying his workplace.
localizations.lestrade_2:
Get out!
You’re wasting my time!
localizations.end_title: Sherlock Holmes - The Burnt Angels Case
localizations.end: You are now at the banks of the Thames, your feet deep in water, sneaking into the tunnel which leads to the London sewers. After a few minutes walk, you come to a dead-end. You spot Red Henry, his back turned towards you, looking down. He seems willing to accept his fate. Without further ado, you handcuff him, and take him to Scotland Yard. When you exit the tunnel, you come face to face with Sherlock Holmes, who seems to have solved the case long ago. He gives you what appears to be a look of approval.
localizations.machine_14_jack_desc:
Admitted following
an arrest for
shoplifting.
localizations.machine_14_henry1_desc:
Abandoned by his mother.
His father in jail.
Does not know how to read.
localizations.machine_14_henry2_desc: Became mute after his parents disappeared.
localizations.machine_14_henry3_desc: Admitted with superficial burns. Refused to be examined by a physician.
localizations.machine_14_henry4_desc:
Raised by his aunt.
Never knew his parents.
localizations.machine_14_henry_intro: During these years, 4 children going by the name of Henry lived at the orphanage. Here are their files.
localizations.machine_14_error_date: There are a lot of files in these archives. You could spend months reviewing them all.
localizations.machine_14_error_noentry: There are no files matching your search in the archives.
localizations.machine_2_pantalon_desc: Mud residues at knee level. Often gets wet.
localizations.machine_2_plume_desc: The feather is quite large. Certainly that of a seagull.
localizations.machine_2_veste_desc: Rodents seem to have been chewing on his jacket.
